steps.title: Identify the evaluative language
steps.content: The underlined portion says Oliveira and colleagues capitalized on the research potential of a vast but underused source of insight. That comments on why their use of historical DNA matters.

steps.title: Reject unsupported functions
steps.content: Choice A says the portion lists applications, but it does not. Choice C shifts to the study's findings about flora. Choice D says it explains why the method is not widely used, which is not the point.

steps.title: State the function
steps.content: The underlined portion evaluates the significance of the study's approach by describing historical DNA as a valuable, underused source. The answer is B.
